notes.anchor: unless the Epirote correspondence is holding you up
notes.note: Atticus owned a large estate at Buthrotum in Epirus (north-west Greece) and conducted regular business correspondence about it. Cicero allows that this may delay Atticus's afternoon letter and tactfully does not press.

notes.anchor: I have sent over to you the letters to Marcianus and to Montanus
notes.note: Cicero encloses two of his own letters --- one to Marcianus, an intermediary, and one to L. Tullius Montanus regarding the latter's debt troubles --- for Atticus to forward in his outgoing packet. Routing private letters through Atticus's courier service was standard practice.
